trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Italy exports machinery, luxury goods, chemicals, and food; South Korea exports electronics, vehicles, and ships. Italian fashion and food are popular in South Korea. Korean manufacturing firms source Italian specialty machinery. Korea's shipbuilding industry competes with Italian luxury yacht sector.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and South Korea enjoy warm diplomatic relations. Both are liberal democracies, OECD members, and important middle powers. Diplomatic contacts have grown with increasing economic integration. Cultural ties through Italian gastronomy, fashion, and design have strong following in South Korea.

military.summary: Limited but growing defense cooperation. Italy is a NATO member; South Korea is a US treaty ally. Some defense technology interest. Italy has participated in multilateral exercises in the Indo-Pacific. No bilateral defense agreement.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Korean conglomerates (Samsung, Hyundai, LG) have Italian operations; Italian luxury firms have significant Korean retail presence. Growing investment in manufacturing partnerships.

energy.summary: Both are energy importers. Some cooperation on energy technology and LNG supply chains. No direct bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation in engineering, automotive technology, and materials science. Joint academic programs exist. Italian universities have exchange programs with Korean counterparts.
